description:
You are deploying a Linux-based Home Theater PC (HTPC). The client wants to watch their 4:3 vintage films stretched to 16:9 fullscreen, with large yellow English subtitles, and no filename overlay. Modify VLC Media Player's persistent preferences (Tools > Preferences, ensuring you check 'All' settings if necessary, or by directly editing ~/.config/vlc/vlcrc) to meet these exact requirements:
1. Playback must always start in fullscreen mode.
2. Force the display aspect ratio to 16:9.
3. Disable the video title/filename popup that appears at the start of playback.
4. Set the preferred/default subtitle language to English ('en' or 'English').
5. Set the default subtitle text color to Yellow.
6. Set the default subtitle font size to Large.

Verify your setup by playing the test video at /home/ga/Videos/vintage_film.mkv. Take a fullscreen screenshot during playback (using VLC's snapshot tool or scrot) and save/rename it as /home/ga/Pictures/vlc/htpc_proof.png.

Finally, create a JSON manifest at /home/ga/Documents/htpc_config.json documenting the changes with the following exact keys and your applied values:
{
  "client": "HTPC Setup",
  "configurations": {
    "fullscreen": true,
    "aspect_ratio": "16:9",
    "osd_title_disabled": true,
    "subtitle_language": "en",
    "subtitle_color": "yellow",
    "subtitle_size": "large"
  }
}
